description_long: This guard rail consists of two parallel horizontal rails supported by a series of evenly spaced, turned balusters, all framed by robust vertical posts at each end. The posts are capped with pointed finials, adding a decorative touch reminiscent of traditional wrought iron or wooden fencing. The main structure appears to be painted a dark green, while the balusters are a contrasting natural wood color, giving the rail a visually appealing two-tone finish. The construction suggests a sturdy, weight-bearing design suitable for demarcating boundaries or providing safety along walkways, parks, or residential areas. The ends of the rail are blunt and squared, with no visible flaring or energy-absorbing features. There are no reflective markings or panels present on the surface.
description: A two-tone guard rail with dark green posts and rails, and natural wood-colored balusters, featuring pointed finials and a sturdy, decorative design.
description_view_0: Front view showing both horizontal rails, all balusters, and the decorative pointed finials on the posts.
description_view_1: Side profile view, revealing the thinness of the rails and posts, with minimal visible detail except the finial tips.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the full arrangement of balusters and posts, but from the opposite side.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile, again showing the narrow edge of the structure and the finial tips.
